title: Daily Market Digest
subtitle: May 08, 2026: Geopolitical Tensions Weigh on Global Markets, US Futures Mixed

items.headline: Global Markets Retreat Amid Escalating Iran-US Tensions; US Futures Show Mixed Signals
items.summary: World shares and oil prices pulled back on Friday, May 8, 2026, as the fragile ceasefire with Iran was strained by missile and drone attacks and subsequent U.S. retaliatory strikes on Iranian military facilities. U.S. stock futures, however, presented a mixed picture, with S&P 500 and Nasdaq futures edging higher, while Dow futures showed gains. Investors are closely monitoring the conflict as negotiations between the U.S. and Iran to end the war make limited progress. This digest provides market insights and is not financial advice.

items.statContext: Futures trading on Friday, May 8. S&P 500 fell 0.4% to 7,337.11 on Thursday.
Futures trading on Friday, May 8. Nasdaq composite dropped 0.1% to 25,806.20 on Thursday.
Futures trading on Friday, May 8, gaining 156 points. Dow Industrials slipped 0.6% to 49,596.97 on Thursday.

items.headline: Datadog Leads Gainers on Strong Earnings
items.summary: Datadog ($DDOG) leaped 31.3% on Thursday, leading the U.S. market after the monitoring and security platform for cloud applications topped analysts' expectations for profit in its latest quarter.

items.headline: Axon Enterprise Rallies on Raised Revenue Forecast
items.summary: Axon Enterprise ($AXON) rallied 10.6% on Thursday after raising its forecast for revenue this year, partly due to significant growth in its counter-drone products.

items.headline: Shake Shack Tumbles on Disappointing Results
items.summary: Shake Shack ($SHAK) tumbled 28.3% on Thursday after its results for the latest quarter fell significantly below analysts' expectations.

items.headline: Whirlpool Sinks Following Weaker-Than-Expected Earnings
items.summary: Whirlpool ($WHR) sank 11.9% on Thursday after reporting much weaker results than analysts expected for its latest quarter.

items.headline: Geopolitical Tensions Elevate Oil Prices and Market Caution
items.summary: Renewed missile and drone attacks near the Strait of Hormuz, followed by U.S. retaliatory strikes, have heightened tensions between the US and Iran, straining an already fragile ceasefire. This geopolitical uncertainty has led to volatile oil prices, with Brent crude dipping 0.2% to $99.84 per barrel and benchmark U.S. crude falling 0.5% to $94.34 a barrel.

items.headline: April Jobs Report: Nonfarm Payrolls Edge Up, Unemployment Rate Holds Steady
items.summary: The U.S. Bureau of Labor Statistics reported today that total nonfarm payroll employment edged up by 115,000 in April, and the unemployment rate remained unchanged at 4.3 percent.

items.headline: Technology Leads Sector Performance Amid Risk-On Sentiment
items.summary: In sector analysis, Technology (XLK) is currently leading, with Real Estate (XLRE) and Consumer Staples (XLP) also showing strong performance. Conversely, Healthcare (XLV) and Financials (XLF) are among the lagging sectors, indicating a 'risk-on' sentiment where investors are favoring growth-oriented plays in a stable economic outlook.

items.headline: PPL Corporation Reports Solid Q1 Earnings, Reaffirms Full-Year Guidance
items.summary: PPL Corporation ($PPL) announced solid first-quarter 2026 earnings, with reported GAAP earnings per share (EPS) of $0.60 and ongoing EPS of $0.63, both up from the previous year. The company reaffirmed its full-year 2026 ongoing earnings forecast of $1.90 to $1.98 per share and its 6% to 8% annual EPS growth target through at least 2029.

items.headline: ANI Pharmaceuticals Exceeds Q1 Expectations, Raises 2026 Guidance
items.summary: ANI Pharmaceuticals, Inc. ($ANIP) reported strong first-quarter 2026 financial results, with total net revenues reaching $237.5 million, marking a 20.5% increase year-over-year. The company has subsequently raised its 2026 financial guidance and authorized a new $100.0 million share repurchase program.
